options.label: A
options.text: A force that helps objects move faster

options.label: B
options.text: A force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact

options.label: C
options.text: A force that only acts on smooth surfaces

options.label: D
options.text: A force that pulls objects downward

explanation: Friction is a force that acts between two surfaces when they rub against each other, and it always opposes the direction of motion. It's why it's hard to slide things across rough surfaces.
